title: Indulgent Crispy Dutch Chicken Tagine
description: A smoky, low‑carb Dutch‑inspired chicken tagine with coconut milk and a crispy coating that melts in the mouth.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200C and heat a dry skillet over medium‑high heat.
Pat the chicken thighs dry and season both sides with smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, cayenne, salt, and pepper.
Sear the chicken in the hot skillet until golden and slightly crispy, about 3 minutes per side, then transfer to a baking sheet.
In the same skillet, pour in coconut milk and chicken broth, bring to a gentle simmer, and stir in lemon juice.
Return the seared chicken to the skillet, spoon some sauce over each piece, and cover with a lid or aluminum foil.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 20 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Remove the skillet, discard the lid, and simmer the remaining sauce on the stovetop until it thickens, about 5 minutes.
Stir in chopped kale during the last 2 minutes of simmering until wilted.
Plate the chicken, drizzle with the reduced sauce, and garnish with fresh cilantro.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
